title: Reading a Word with a Beginning Blend

steps.instruction: Look at this word: stop. The first two consonants are next to each other.
steps.explanation: A blend is two consonants that each keep their own sound but are said quickly together. Unlike a digraph, you can still hear both sounds.
In a blend, you hear both sounds: /s/ and /t/. They slide together smoothly.

steps.instruction: Now blend /st/ with the vowel and final consonant.
steps.explanation: The word is 'stop'. Other beginning blends include bl (black), cr (crab), gr (green), and fl (flag).

title: Reading a Word with an Ending Blend

steps.instruction: Look at this word: best. Notice the last two consonants.
steps.explanation: This word ends with two consonants: 's' and 't'. These form an ending blend.

steps.instruction: Start with the first letter and vowel: b-e.
steps.explanation: The 'e' makes its short sound /e/ (like in 'bed').
The word is 'best'. Common ending blends include -nd (band), -mp (jump), -nk (pink), and -lt (felt).
